market_summary: Mixed-to-fragile regime for the 30-day horizon: SPY -3.02% 1m and QQQ -3.23% 1m, both below MA50/MA200, while IWM is relatively firmer at -1.54% 1m and still above MA200; leadership remains narrow in Energy, defense, and select semicap.

Current book remains broadly regime-consistent: XOM, CVX, LMT, KLAC, and COST have the cleanest pack-derived trend/risk profile; GOOGL and TSM retain medium-term strength despite softer 1m tape, while NVDA remains the weakest current holding on near-term trend support.

No-go for an off-cycle rebalance today: cadence is not due, the prior portfolio must remain unchanged, and the qualitative refresh did not verify severe thesis damage strong enough to justify breaking weekly spacing.

thesis_damage_flags.why: Pack shows weaker short-horizon trend than the rest of the book (-3.10% 1m, -5.93% 3m, below MA50 and MA200). Fundamentals remain elite, but valuation is still rich at ~38x P/E and ~21x sales, so the name remains vulnerable if AI leadership does not re-broaden.

Pack momentum is still acceptable versus index, but it remains below MA50 and its latest fundamentals feed contains some incomplete/unstable ratio fields; use caution on valuation precision. No verified 7-day thesis break was confirmed, so damage is moderate rather than severe.

Trend remains strong, but fundamentals are cyclical rather than improving: TTM revenue growth about -0.3% and earnings growth about -3.7%. If oil leadership rolls over, recent outperformance could fade quickly.

Strong relative trend persists, but underlying fundamental growth is soft to negative in recent TTM snapshots and payout looks stretched in parts of the data set. That is a watch item, not a sell signal today.

thesis: Catalyst: none strong enough to justify an off-cycle trade before the weekly rebalance window.
Factor edge: preserving exposure to existing winners in Energy, defense, semicap, and defensive quality avoids paying turnover into a still-fragile tape.
Variant view: the market could snap risk-on and favor higher-beta laggards, but current evidence does not clear the bar for changing the paper book today.
risks: Macro: a sharp regime shift in rates, oil, or index breadth could quickly change the relative ranking of current holdings.
Idiosyncratic: NVDA and other growth names remain vulnerable to any negative AI-demand or guidance surprise; Energy names remain exposed to commodity reversals.
Execution/liquidity: low for current large-cap holdings, but unnecessary off-cycle turnover would add slippage/opportunity-cost without a verified edge.
falsifiable_checks: By the next weekly checkpoint, if NVDA reclaims both MA50 and MA200 while sustaining better 2-week relative performance than SPY, the current 'hold/no-trade' stance becomes less conservative.
By the next weekly checkpoint, if XOM and CVX lose relative-strength leadership and close below MA50 while defensive quality no longer offsets, reassess whether the book still fits regime.
trade_of_the_day.why_now: Base: mixed-to-fragile regime with narrow leadership argues for patience, and the expected alpha from an off-cycle change does not clearly exceed trading-cost drag. Bull: existing winners in Energy/defense/semicap continue to carry the book without extra turnover. Bear: risk-on breadth broadens and lagging mega-cap growth rebounds faster than the current portfolio. Even in that case, spacing discipline is still favored today because no severe verified thesis break was found.

constraints_check.notes: Appendix: factor highlights=XOM/KLAC/LMT/COST strongest blend of trend and quality; NVDA weakest near-term trend but still high-quality; GOOGL data partly incomplete so confidence moderated. Risk budget=8 names, largest position 16%, Energy 30%, IT 38%, Crypto 0%. Scenario stress=equity index shock hurts NVDA/TSM/KLAC/GOOGL most, oil shock hurts XOM/CVX, rates shock modestly pressures COST/quality, USD shock is mixed with TSM ADR sensitivity; no single sector breaches limit. Execution plan=no trade today; preserve weekly cadence. Monitoring/kill-switch=watch NVDA trend repair/failure, Energy leadership rollover, and any severe verified earnings/guidance deterioration in current holdings; UNKNOWN for 24-72h news detail where not verified in the refresh.
